volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 4.3.554 - Section 5.2.617)

sections.label: Section 4.3.554
sections.translation: How — farewell ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.554b
sections.original: Aut tu aegrota aetatem , si lubet , per me quidem .—
sections.translation: Fare you ill then , if you choose ,— aye , all your life , so far as I ’ m concerned . ( Exit .)

sections.label: Section 4.3.555
sections.original: Quid ego nunc faciam ? quid refert me fecisse regibus
sections.translation: What shall I do now ? Of what use is it that I have caused kings to obey me , if this obscure fellow [*] is this day to laugh at me ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.557
sections.original: Quoi homini di sunt propitii , ei non esse iratos puto .
sections.translation: The man to whom the Gods are propitious , they cannot , I think , be angered with him . After I ad finished my devotions , it then came into my mind , lest the banker should abscond , to go fetch the money ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.560
sections.original: ut ego potius comedim quam ille .
sections.translation: that I may make good cheer rather than he .

sections.label: Section 4.4.560b
sections.translation: I had left my compliments for you at your house .

sections.label: Section 4.4.561
sections.original: Therapontigone Platagidore , salve ; salvos quom advenis
sections.translation: Therapontigonus Platagidorus , save you ; since you are come safe to Epidaurus this day , at my house — you won ’ t lick up one grain of salt [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.563
sections.original: Bene vocas , verum locata res est — ut male sit tibi .
sections.translation: You give me a kind invitation ; things , however , are in a train for it to go badly with yourself . But how fares my purchase at your house ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.564b
sections.translation: Why , not at my house at all .

sections.label: Section 4.4.565
sections.original: ne facias testis , neque equidem debeo quicquam .
sections.translation: Don ’ t be bringing your witnesses — assuredly , I don ’ t owe you anything .

sections.label: Section 4.4.565b
sections.translation: How ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.566
sections.translation: What I was bound on oath to do , I ’ ve done .

sections.label: Section 4.4.566b
sections.translation: Will you give me up the girl or not , before I spit you with this sabre of mine , you whip-scoundrel ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.568
sections.original: Vapulare ego te vehementer iubeo : ne me territes .
sections.translation: I bid you go to perdition with all my heart ; don ’ t you be terrifying me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.569
sections.original: illa abductast , tu auferere hinc a me , si perges mihi
sections.translation: She has been carried off ; you shall be carried off hence away from me , beyond a doubt , if you persist in abusing me , to whom I owe nothing but a punishing .

sections.label: Section 4.4.571
sections.translation: What , threaten me with a punishing ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.571b
sections.original: Atque edepol non minitabor , sed dabo ,
sections.translation: Aye , and by my troth , I ’ ll not be threatening , but I ’ ll give it , if you persist in being impertinent to me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.572b
sections.translation: A Procurer , forsooth , threatening me ; and are my combats in battle , so many in number , lying trodden under foot ? But so may my sabre and my shield ⋯

sections.label: Section 4.4.575
sections.original: bene iuvent pugnantem in acie : nisi mi virgo redditur ,
sections.translation: trustily aid me when fighting in the field ; unless the girl is restored to me , I ’ ll at once cause the ants to carry you away piecemeal from this spot .

sections.label: Section 4.4.577
sections.original: At ita me volsellae , pecten , speculum , calamistrum meum
sections.translation: And so may my tweezers [*] , my comb , my looking-glass , my crisping-iron , and my hair-scissors and scrubbing-towel love me well ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.580
sections.original: non pluris facio quam ancillam meam quae latrinam lavat .
sections.translation: I don ’ t value your high-sounding words , nor these big threats of yours , a bit the more than my servant-girl that washes out my sink . I ’ ve given her up to him who brought the money from you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.581b
sections.translation: What person was that ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.582
sections.original: Tuom libertum ésse aiebat sese Summanum .
sections.translation: He said that he was Summanus , your freed-man .

sections.label: Section 4.4.582b
sections.translation: Mine ? You don ’ t say so ; i ’ faith , it ’ s that Curculio that has put a trick upon me , when I think upon it ; he stole my ring from me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.584b
sections.translation: The Captain has been finely appointed to a cashiered company [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.586
sections.translation: Where now shall I find Curculio [*] ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.586b
sections.translation: In some wheat with the greatest ease I ’ ll make you find even five hundred Curculios instead of one . Therefore I ’ m off ; fare you well , and my service to you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.588b
sections.translation: Fare you ill , a plague attend you . What shall I do ? Shall I stop or go away ? That I should have been imposed upon in this way !

sections.label: Section 4.4.590
sections.original: cupio dare mercedem qui illunc ubi sit commonstret mihi .
sections.translation: I ’ d like to give a reward to him who would point out that fellow to me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.591
sections.original: Antiquom poëtam audivi scripsisse in tragoedia ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve heard that an ancient Poet [*] wrote in a Tragedy , that two women are worse than one ; such is the fact . But a more crafty woman than is this mistress of Phaedromus , I never saw or heard of , nor , i ’ faith , can one be spoken of or imagined ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.595
sections.original: peior quam haec est : quae ubi me habere hunc conspicatast anulum ,
sections.translation: she , as soon as ever she saw that I had got this ring , asked me whence I got it . Why do you ask that ? said I . Because I ’ ve need to know , said she . I said I wouldn ’ t tell her . To get it away , she seized hold of my hand with her teeth . With much ado I betook me out of doors , and made my escape . Away with this slut [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.599
sections.translation: My Phaedromus , make haste .

sections.label: Section 5.2.599b
sections.translation: Make haste for what ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.599c
sections.translation: That you mayn ’ t let the Parasite escape ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.600
sections.translation: the affair ’ s of consequence .

sections.label: Section 5.2.600b
sections.original: Nulla est mihi , nam quam habui absumpsi celeriter .
sections.translation: Nothing have I [*] of consequence ; for what I did have I quickly made away with ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.601
sections.translation: I ’ ve got him ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.601b
sections.translation: what ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.601c
sections.translation: Ask him whence he got that ring : my father used to wear it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.602b
sections.translation: Why , troth , and so did my aunt .

sections.label: Section 5.2.603
sections.translation: My mother gave it him to wear .

sections.label: Section 5.2.603b
sections.translation: And your father , in his turn , to me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.604
sections.translation: You are talking nonsense .

sections.label: Section 5.2.604b
sections.original: Soleo , nam propter eas vivo facilius .
sections.translation: I ’ m in the habit ; for by reason of it I make a livelihood the more easily . What is the matter now ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.605
sections.original: Quid nunc ? obsecro , parentes ne meos mihi prohibeas .
sections.translation: I entreat you , don ’ t be keeping me from finding my parents .

sections.label: Section 5.2.606
sections.original: Quid ego ? sub gemmane abstrusos habeo tuam matrem et patrem ?
sections.translation: How can I ? Have I got your father and mother hidden beneath the stone ? ( Holding up the ring .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.607
sections.translation: I was born a free woman .

sections.label: Section 5.2.607b
sections.original: Et alii multi qui nunc serviunt .
sections.translation: And so were many others , who are now in servitude .

sections.label: Section 5.2.608
sections.translation: Really now , I ’ m vexed .

sections.label: Section 5.2.608b
sections.original: Dixi equidem tibi , unde ad me hic pervenerit .
sections.translation: For my part , I ’ ve told you how this came into my possession . How often must you be told ? I cheated the Captain at dice , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.610
sections.original: Salvos sum , eccum quem quaerebam . quid agis , bone vir ?
sections.translation: I ’ m all right ; see , here he is whom I was looking for . How do you do , good sir ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.610b
sections.translation: I hear you ; three casts of the dice , if you like , even for that scarf of yours .

sections.label: Section 5.2.611b
sections.original: Quin tu is in malam crucem
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go to very perdition , with your casts and catch-pennies [*] ? Either give me back the money or the young woman ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.613
sections.original: Quod argentum , quas tu mihi tricas narras ? quam tu virginem
sections.translation: What money ? what rubbish are you talking about to me ? What young woman are you demanding back of me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.614b
sections.original: Quam ab lenone ábduxti hodie , scelus viri .
sections.translation: The one that you took away this day from the Procurer , you scoundrelly fellow .

sections.label: Section 5.2.615
sections.translation: I took none away .

sections.label: Section 5.2.615b
sections.translation: Why , surely , I see her . There she is . ( Pointing to PLANESIUM .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.615c
sections.translation: This young woman is free .

sections.label: Section 5.2.616
sections.original: Mean ancilla libera ut sit , quam ego numquam emisi manu ?
sections.translation: What , my female slave free , to whom I have never given her liberty ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.617
sections.original: Quis tibi hanc dedit mancipio ? aut unde emisti ? fac sciam .
sections.translation: Who gave you possession of her , or of whom did you buy her ? Let me know that ?
